document.title: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation report for ADD, a wired or wireless digital flat panel X-ray detector, prepared for MDD/CE clinical evaluation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document clinical evaluation, equivalence, safety, performance, benefit-risk, and PMS/PMCF considerations for ADD under MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4 and MDD Essential Requirements.
keyPoints: ADD is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The evaluation relies on literature, equivalent predicate devices, PMS documents, risk management documents, and pre-clinical evidence.
The report concludes that benefits outweigh residual risks and that safety and performance are supported by clinical evaluation.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is regarded as an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: MDD Class IIa classification
concepts.evidence: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: We have demonstrated equivalence according to the Demonstration of Equivalence requirements of MEDDEV 2.7 / 1 Rev.4

concepts.name: Essential Requirements
concepts.evidence: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed

concepts.name: Post Market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: The PMS report includes: Customer Complaints Analysis (Latest 5 years), Adverse Events, Experience with Similar Devices Made by the Same or Different Manufacturer, Literature Reviews, PMCF Study

concepts.name: Benefit-risk profile
concepts.evidence: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

evidence.claim: The document is Clinical Evaluation Report A-TCF-5-060 Rev.2 for ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Report for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-060 (Rev.2)
Doc. No. A-TCF-5-060 Rev. No. 2 Date 2021.04.02.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer H&abyz Co.,Ltd. Address 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: ADD is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD are wired or wireless digital flat panel detector that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: ADD uses TFT glass, CsI scintillators, amorphous silicon technology, photodiodes, and TFT switching elements.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD detector utilize a combination of propriety TFT glass and scintillators (CsI)... The flat panel sensors of the ADD are fabricated using thin film technology based on amorphous silicon technology.

evidence.claim: ADD is classified as Class IIa under Rule 16 of Annex IX of MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Contraindications include limitations in X-rays due to health issues, mammography, direct cardiac application, infants, and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The target user group includes radiologists and users able to read and understand the manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Knowledge: User must be read and understand user manual... Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: ADD safety and performance are assessed against MDD Essential Requirements 1, 3, and 6.
evidence.evidenceText: The conformity to the following Essential Requirements (MDD 93/42/EEC Annex I) is assessed: Essential Requirement 1: Safety Requirements; Essential Requirement 3: Performance Requirements; Essential Requirement 6: Side-effect Requirements

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation was performed using currently available scientific literature on equivalent products, not clinical investigations.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on scientific literature currently available A... Clinical evaluations were performed using the currently available scientific literatures on equivalent products. Based on Clinical investigations made N/A

evidence.claim: The report identifies DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, and S4335AW as devices used for clinical evaluation data.
evidence.evidenceText: We used as the clinical evaluation data the follow device, -DRX-1, CXDI-31, CXDI-70C, CXDI-11, S4335AW

evidence.claim: The report states that ADD has no sales record during the assessed PMS period.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer Complaints Analysis There is no sales record during this period Adverse Events There is no sales record during the period.

evidence.claim: The literature search used PUBMED and Science Direct, with search dates from 2020.01.05 to 2020.06.15.
evidence.evidenceText: The “PUBMED” and “Science Direct” were utilized to search for literature... Date of Search: 2020.01.05 ~ 2020.06.15

evidence.claim: The report concludes that ADD can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.
evidence.evidenceText: Considering the literature review, safety & performance test and comparison to similar devices, we concluded that this device can be expected to exhibit the claimed technical performance and potential clinical effects.

evidence.claim: The report concludes that risks associated with use of ADD are acceptable when weighed against patient benefits.
evidence.evidenceText: The risks associated with the use of the device are acceptable when weighed against the benefits to the patient.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal says MFDS/domestic, but the document text primarily supports EU/MDD/CE context; MFDS authority is not clearly established in the report text.
The source path and productSignal mention HAD1417MCW or HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document body identifies the evaluated model as ADD.
Document date and final release dates conflict: pages show Rev.2 Date 2021.04.02, while the Dates and Signatures section shows 2020.09.01.
Equivalent device naming has an inconsistency: text refers to S4335AW and later S4336AW.
OCR/text quality contains typographical issues such as 'propriety TFT', 'commuted Radiography', 'wirless', and formatting artifacts, but core metadata is readable.
